title: lychnis quadrifida
intro: Lychnis quadrifida, also known as the four-leafed catchfly, is a plant species in the caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its distinctive four-leafed leaves and red or pink flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lychnis quadrifida include alleviating bronchial spasms, reducing inflammation, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically harvested, dried, and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
